trust-green.note: Fill only (buttons, under white text). Unchanged across themes — a white-on-green contrast pair doesn't depend on page background.

accent.note: Trust Green as on-surface text/border/ring, not fill. Diverges from trust-green in dark mode (brighter, for contrast against a dark surface). --color-fresh is the same variable as this token.

stale-gray.note: Unchanged in dark mode — already light enough to read well against a dark surface (~7.2:1).

border.note: Dark value is brighter than a 'subtle divider' instinct suggests — needed to clear the ~3:1 WCAG 1.4.11 non-text contrast minimum against the dark surface.

user-location.note: Map 'this is you' marker only. Deliberately not Trust Green — reusing brand/fresh green here would blur the Trust Green Rule's 'confirmed' signal. Unchanged across themes; the map canvas itself stays light-styled regardless of app theme.

headline.purpose: Page-level titles (auth screens, report flow steps).

body.purpose: Input text, paragraph copy, primary button labels — never below 16px on real inputs.

label.purpose: Form labels, ghost/secondary button text, chip text.

caption.purpose: Hints, freshness timestamps, helper copy under inputs.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Search bar, radius pill, fetch-error card — a light lift to read as 'on top of the map.'

shadows.name: menu
shadows.purpose: Account avatar chip, account dropdown, toast stack.

shadows.name: sheet
shadows.purpose: The two bottom sheets — strongest shadow in the system.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Full-width solid action button — form submits, confirmations.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1a5c3a; color: #ffffff; font: 600 1rem/1.5 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; border: none; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 12px 16px; width: 100%; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1a5c3a; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { opacity: 0.85; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Pill CTA
components.description: Standalone high-commitment CTA — report FAB, empty-state and success-screen actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-pill { background: #1a5c3a; color: #ffffff; font: 600 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; border: none; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 12px 32px; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-pill:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1a5c3a; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-pill:active { opacity: 0.85; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Text-only secondary action — Cancelar, Editar, Tentar novamente.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #6b7280; font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; border: none; padding: 12px 16px; min-height: 44px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1a5c3a; outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 0.75rem; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #1a1a1a; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Labeled text field — label always above, never placeholder-only.
components.css: .ds-field-label { display: block; font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; color: #1a1a1a; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-field-input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; border: 1px solid #e5e5e0; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 12px 16px; font: 400 1rem/1.5 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; color: #1a1a1a; background: #ffffff; } .ds-field-input::placeholder { color: #6b7280; } .ds-field-input:focus { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #1a5c3a; }

components.name: Selectable Chip
components.description: Border-only selectable option — flag reasons, nearby-place picks.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: block; width: 100%; text-align: left; background: #ffffff; color: #1a1a1a; border: 1px solid #e5e5e0; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 12px 16px; min-height: 44px; font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; cursor: pointer; } .ds-chip:hover { background: #f7f7f5; } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1a5c3a; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-chip-selected { border-color: #1a5c3a; background: rgba(26, 92, 58, 0.1); color: #1a5c3a; } /* light-mode snapshot — selected state uses the accent token, which brightens to #3ea873 in dark mode */

components.name: Bottom Sheet
components.description: Shared dialog primitive for 'sheet over the map' surfaces — place details, flag/report.
components.html:
Denunciar

Conteúdo do sheet…
components.css: .ds-sheet { position: relative; background: #ffffff; border-radius: 1rem 1rem 0 0; box-shadow: 0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,0.1), 0 8px 10px -6px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); padding: 24px; } .ds-sheet-title { font: 700 1.125rem/1.375 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; color: #1a1a1a; margin: 0 0 16px; } .ds-sheet-body { font: 400 1rem/1.5 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; color: #1a1a1a; margin: 0; }

components.name: Summary Card
components.description: Flat label/value summary — the report confirm-step recap.
components.html:
ProdutoArroz 5kg
PreçoR$ 24,90
components.css: .ds-card { background: #f7f7f5; border-radius: 1rem; padding: 16px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; } .ds-card-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; gap: 12px; } .ds-card-label { font: 400 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; color: #6b7280; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-card-value { font: 500 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; color: #1a1a1a; text-align: right; }

components.name: Freshness Label
components.description: PRODUCT.md's first-class trust signal — color- and text-coded Report age, used on markers and list rows alike.
components.css: .ds-freshness { font: 400 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; } .ds-freshness-fresh { color: #1a5c3a; } .ds-freshness-aging { color: #b45309; } .ds-freshness-stale { color: #9ca3af; }

narrative.overview:
Aonde Tem is built to feel like a trusted local who already checked the shelves for you — plain, quick, and reliable rather than clever or performative. Every screen exists to answer one of two questions fast: where is it, and how fresh is that answer — so the interface stays out of the way of the map, the price, and the timestamp. The voice is Brazilian-Portuguese-first, friendly and practical, the way a helpful neighbor talks, never markety or hype-driven.

This system explicitly rejects the generic AI-tool look: no purple/indigo gradients, no glassmorphism, no neon-on-black dashboards, no fake-metric hero sections, no gradient text, no side-stripe card borders, no emoji standing in for real iconography, and no over-rounded 'friendly SaaS' everything. It is a single-brand-green, mostly-flat, map-first tool, not a marketing surface.
keyCharacteristics: Map-first: the interface is a thin, legible layer of cards and sheets floating over MapLibre, never burying it.
One accent color doing double duty: Trust Green is both the brand identity and the 'fresh Report' state color.
Flat at rest, shadowed only when floating above the map (search bar, FAB, bottom sheets, toasts).
One type family, fixed rem scale, five weight/size roles — no display face, no fluid clamp() sizing.
44px minimum touch targets as a near-universal, deliberate discipline (mobile, one-handed, on the move).
Light and dark themes, user-toggled — every token pair is contrast-verified independently in each theme.

rules.name: The Trust Green Rule
rules.body: The 'fresh' state color and the on-surface accent color are the same CSS variable on purpose (--color-fresh: var(--color-accent)). A user should feel the same 'this is good, act on it' signal whether green appears on a Report's timestamp or a link. The fill token (--color-brand, buttons) is allowed to diverge from the accent token across themes — a button's contrast pair is against white text, not the page.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Trust Green (fill or accent) is the only saturated color on any given screen. Aging Amber and Stale Gray are muted, functional, and only ever apply to freshness labels.

rules.name: The Single-Family Rule
rules.body: Product UI doesn't need a display/body pairing. One well-tuned system sans carries headings, labels, body, and data.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadow means 'this is temporarily covering the map or the page,' never 'this card is fancier than that card.' A shadow on a static content card is a bug, not a style choice.

rules.name: The Two-Radius Rule
rules.body: rounded-control (12px) is for anything living inside a form, list, or sheet. rounded-full is reserved for a small, deliberate set of standalone commitment-moment CTAs, the account avatar, and plain circular badges/icons. Both --radius-control and --radius-sheet are real @theme tokens in index.css, backing genuine rounded-control / rounded-sheet / rounded-t-sheet utility classes.

rules.name: The MapLibre Escape Hatch Rule
rules.body: MapLibre's paint API needs literal color strings — it cannot consume CSS custom properties. apps/web/src/features/map/model/map-colors.ts holds the one permitted set of duplicated hex literals (MAP_COLORS.brand/.fresh/.aging/.stale), each commented against its index.css counterpart. Never inline a fresh hex string into a MapLibre paint object — import from MAP_COLORS instead. DOM content over the map (markers, popups) is ordinary CSS and should reference the real token (e.g. bg-user-location), not this module.

dos: Do keep Trust Green as the only saturated accent color on any screen — Aging Amber and Stale Gray are functional freshness labels only, never decorative.
Do use the system font stack everywhere; never load a webfont for this product.
Do give every button, chip, and icon-only control a 44×44px minimum hit target.
Do reserve shadow for things floating above the map (search bar, FABs, dropdown, toasts, bottom sheets) — flat everywhere else.
Do build any new 'sheet over content' surface on the shared BottomSheet primitive (dialog role, focus trap, Escape-to-close), not a bespoke position: absolute div.

Do use text-accent / border-accent / ring-accent / bg-accent/10 when Trust Green appears as text, a border, a focus ring, or a translucent tint directly on a surface. Reserve bg-brand for solid fills under white text.
Do reference the semantic z-index scale (z-dropdown 20 → z-sticky 30 → z-modal-backdrop 40 → z-modal 50 → z-toast 60 → z-tooltip 70) via the z-(--z-name) syntax for any new stacking-context element.

Do use rounded-control / rounded-sheet / rounded-t-sheet for any new rectangular control or card — both are real --radius-* tokens in index.css, not raw Tailwind numbers. Reach for bare rounded-full only for the Pill CTA tier or a plain circular badge/icon.

Do import from map-colors.ts's MAP_COLORS for any MapLibre paint-layer color — never inline a fresh hex literal into a paint object. DOM content drawn over the map should reference the real CSS token instead (e.g. bg-user-location).
donts: Don't use purple/indigo gradients, glassmorphism, or neon-on-black 'dashboard' looks — explicitly banned by docs/PRODUCT.md.
Don't write generic SaaS hero sections, 'Boost your productivity' energy, or fake-metric dashboards — this is a utility, not a marketing surface.
Don't use gradient text, side-stripe card borders, or over-round-everything — a rectangular control gets the Control radius, not a pill, 'for friendliness.'
Don't clutter the map, ship tap targets under 44px, or put low-contrast text over map imagery.

Don't use emoji as primary illustration — docs/PRODUCT.md bans emoji-as-UI outright. EmptyState, ReportPage's success screen, and ErrorBoundary (formerly map/checkmark/warning emoji) now use hand-authored stroke icons matching the app's existing Feather-style SVG vocabulary instead.

Don't write a bare z-dropdown / z-sticky / z-modal / z-toast class (no parens, no var()). Tailwind v4's @theme only recognizes its own token namespaces and silently drops any --z-* custom property declared inside it, so a bare class compiles to nothing and resolves to z-index: auto. Declare the scale as plain :root custom properties and reference them with z-(--z-name).

Don't introduce a second saturated brand fill hue — --color-brand stays one value in both themes. The accent role is expected to diverge across themes; that's --color-accent's job, not a hue proliferation.
